model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: UConn enters this Sweet 16 matchup with a slight statistical advantage, boasting top-10 rankings in both KenPom and Torvik compared to Michigan State's standing near 10th. The Huskies feature an elite, balanced profile anchored by a top-15 defense that should be able to contain the Spartans' highly efficient scoring attack. While Michigan State is a formidable opponent with a top-25 offense, their backcourt depth has been compromised by the season-ending injury to Divine Ugochukwu and lingering availability questions surrounding Jeremy Fears Jr. With a superior defensive unit and overwhelming overall talent depth, UConn is the confident pick to outlast Michigan State and advance to the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: Compare UConn and Michigan State profiles
reasoningStep.summary: UConn has a slight edge in overall efficiency and defense, while Michigan State deals with backcourt depth issues.
evidence: UConn ranks in the top 10 in KenPom/Torvik with a top-15 defense.
Michigan State ranks 10th with a top-25 offense and top-30 defense.
Michigan State lost Divine Ugochukwu for the season and has availability questions around Jeremy Fears Jr.
UConn's superior defense and depth give them the edge.

result.title: Michigan State's Jeremy Fears Jr. shot in leg in hometown
result.snippet: Michigan State men's basketball freshman Jeremy Fears Jr. suffered nonlife-threatening injuries from a gunshot wound near his hometown of Joliet, Illinois, the school announced Saturday.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
